raw_consumer_statement: I made a purchase with my gold Amex card on XX/XX/XXXX. The package was supposed to be delivered to the specified location between 4-6 days, since the date of purchase, as per the website, since I paid for insurance on the package and that was the timeframe that the company provided for insured shipping ( I have proof of this ). On XX/XX/XXXX I was informed that this location ( the location where the package was going to arrive ) was going to permanently close down and that we were going to be there until XX/XX/XXXX ( I was suppose to receive my package by XX/XX/XXXX, the latest, as I paid for insurance on the package ) it was XX/XX/XXXX and my package still wasnt delivered. We permanently closed down this location on XX/XX/XXXX, meaning this location was emptied out and no one was there anymore, by XX/XX/XXXX. I submitted proof to Amex, that the package never arrived and also provided them with a note that was left on the tracking website, by USPS, stating that the location was closed and they cant access it for delivery- this note was left on XX/XX/XXXX. In spite of having all this information, Amex came back to me saying that XXXX responsible for this charge because the company showed proof of delivery on XX/XX/XXXX!!! HOW IS THAT POSSIBLE??! Im showing you paper-proof that this location was and is permanently closed down, since XX/XX/XXXX, and I havent been physically there since XX/XX/XXXX, how can you accept a delivery date of XX/XX/XXXX as a valid delivery date? When Im showing you that I couldnt have possibly received this package because NO ONE WAS LEFT AT THIS LOCATION SINCE XX/XX/XXXX, WHEN ALL KEYS FOR THE PREMISES WERE SUBMITTED TO THE LANDLORD ON XX/XX/XXXX, and the package, until this date, hadnt arrive to the delivery location!!! Amex took the side of the company and refused to give me back the {$100.00}, which I paid using their card, for merchandise which I never received. If Im submitting all documents needed, showing Amex that I never received my goods because the delivery address was no longer available AND Im showing you that the company stated a 4-6 day delivery date and delivered 13 days AFTER their stated delivery date, how can I be responsible for this??? Thats not right. The company should have been held responsible A ) for charging insurance on a package which was delivered without a signature, because I have proof that no one was at this location on XX/XX/XXXX and B ) for delivering past their promised date.
